intro: Trevia tricuspidata, also known as the Three-leafed Milk Bush or Cat's Claw Millettia, is a climbing plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits such as antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties.
